title: Confirm a refund or reversal
preconditions: Both Canonical Transactions exist; the original is an outflow on an owned deposit account; the owner or agent explicitly selected the proposed relationship.
postconditions: The active refund credit belongs in full to exactly one original; one original may have multiple credits only within its magnitude cap; participants have no incompatible active roles; unlinked credits remain ordinary transactions and all source/relationship history remains queryable.
main_flow: 1. The agent retrieves the original outflow, proposed credit, current active category, existing active refund credits, all active roles, and supplies an idempotency identity. 2. The owner or agent explicitly requests a refund/reversal link. 3. Tally validates same account/currency, opposite signs, active participants, no transfer role, that the credit is neither another refund original nor already used as a refund credit, and that cumulative active refund magnitude including this full credit is at most the original magnitude. 4. Tally records the attributable relationship using the credit's entire magnitude without changing either source fact. 5. The credit reduces External Spend in its own Effective Date period and follows the original transaction's current category. 6. Tally returns the relationship and resulting spend effect.
alt_flows: 3a. Account or currency differs, signs do not oppose, a participant is inactive, or cumulative refund exceeds the original -> reject the link
3b. Either participant is an active transfer leg, the credit is already linked to an original, or a refund credit is proposed as an original -> return a stable role/cardinality conflict
4a. The relationship write fails -> leave both transactions and prior relationships unchanged
5a. The original category is later corrected -> apply the linked offset to the new active category
5b. The refund's Effective Date is in a later period -> subtract it in that later period even when the original is outside the period
metadata: {"depth_tier":3,"primary_actor":"P1","trigger":"A credit appears to refund or reverse some or all of an earlier spend transaction.","status":"draft"}
